delta.note: Skills improve assertion pass rate by 63 percentage points on average

findings: siem-search-1 is a weak discriminator: without_skill agent read local reference files and gave a richer answer. Test needs a harder variant that can't be answered from files.
siem-search-2: without_skill produced SQL — fundamental language confusion. Skill critical.
siem-search-3: without_skill produced SQL/pipe join syntax — completely wrong. Skill critical.
yara-l-1: without_skill used invalid operators (==), hallucinated fields (metadata.log_source), and wrong match syntax (max_span). Partial structure correct.
yara-l-2 (perfect discriminator): without_skill misdiagnosed the error entirely (said 'missing match:' instead of 'regex on enumerated field'). With skill: perfect.
detection-1: without_skill invented 'ENABLED - TEST mode' (does not exist) and never mentioned that saving ≠ enabling. Critical workflow gotcha missed.

action_items: Replace siem-search-1 with a harder log type test (e.g., ask for a vendor not in the reference files, or ask which is premium vs standard)
Add an eval testing the stats aggregation data delay gotcha (2-hour delay for newly ingested data)
Add an eval testing the nocase-on-string-literal gotcha
yara-l skills are performing strongly — consider adding multi-stage query eval
